title: US AI Infrastructure Monitor — US AI data-center project + power-deal ledger
description:
An open, source-linked dataset of US AI data-center infrastructure: a ledger of named US AI data-center campuses and their power arrangements, plus companion grid, power-price, interconnection-queue, and siting feeds.

The canonical file, data/projects.json, records named US AI data-center campuses with verified, clickable source URLs, coordinates, capacity (MW), capacity type, status, and power model (generation + grid/BTM/colocated). Each record carries a three-part provenance model — provenance (government / filing / operator / trade_press / analyst), transformation (reported / calculated / estimated / forecast / scenario), and confidence (high / medium / low). Every sources[] entry was opened and checked: supports_claim: true means the page was confirmed to state the operator + ~MW + status. Several headline MW figures are announced/ultimate targets, not confirmed current draw, and are flagged per record via capacity_type, transformation, and a note.

The ledger is also published as flat CSV (data/projects.csv) and geocoded GeoJSON points (data/projects.geojson), both generated from data/projects.json, and is described by a JSON Schema (schemas/projects.schema.json) and an OpenAPI document (api/openapi.yaml).

Curated data is licensed CC BY 4.0. Third-party analyst sources cited per record retain their own terms (marked with provenance: analyst) and are pointers, not relicensed. Suggested attribution: "US AI Infrastructure Monitor — data CC BY 4.0, https://vijay-sachdeva.github.io/us-ai-infra". The accompanying dashboard code is MIT-licensed and lives in the same repository.
notes: Dataset versions track the `version` field in data/projects.json. Curated datasets are CC BY 4.0; third-party analyst sources cited per record retain their own terms (provenance: analyst). Code in the same repository is MIT-licensed.
